observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Management, thru the Local Chief Executive, exhaust all possible remedies available to collect from the persons liable who are separated from the Municipality or deceased.
observations.managementAction: The Management has issued demand letters to the heirs of the accountable officers.
observations.reason:
The balance of the Other Receivables account amounting to P10,747,539.12 remained outstanding as at December 31, 2024.

There was no movement of the account as at December 31, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Municipal Accountant set-up and maintain the subsidiary ledgers of the individual taxpayers to support the recorded balance of Real Property Tax (RPT) Receivable and Special Education Tax (SET) Receivable. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor ensure reconciliation of their respective balances to ascertain the accuracy, validity and reliability of the receivable accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor are currently reconciling their records in relation to the RPT and SET receivables of the LGU.
observations.reason: No subsidiary ledgers of the individual taxpayers were maintained by the LGU to support the recorded balances of RPT and SET Receivables.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Accounting Office submit immediately the lacking disbursement vouchers, together with the supporting documents, and henceforth strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 on the submission of accounts and other reports of the Municipality on or before the set deadline, to avoid administrative sanctions and/or disallowance of said accounts, as provided for in Section 39 (1) & (3) of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Management did not submit the documents within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: The delay in the submission of accounts of the LGU was due to the volume of documents sorted and lack of manpower in the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Ensure the timely reclassification of completed projects from the Construction in Progress accounts to the appropriate Property, Plant and Equipment accounts immediately upon project completion, as required under Section 50, Chapter 3 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units, Volume I;
Recognize the related Depreciation Expense of the asset when it is already available for use, in accordance with Paragraph 71 of IPSAS 17, to reflect accurate balances in the Statement of Financial Position and Statement of Financial Performance; and
Review and adjust the accounting records and compute the depreciation expense of the completed projects from the period the PPE accounts were available for use until CY 2024, thereby ensuring the reliability of the PPE accounts and the reported surplus.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant set-up and maintain the subsidiary ledgers of the individual taxpayers to support the recorded balance of Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor ensure reconciliation of their respective balances to ascertain the accuracy, validity and reliability of the receivable accounts.
We recommended that the Management, thru the Local Chief Executive, exhaust all possible remedies available to collect from the persons liable who are separated from the LGU or deceased.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary journal entries to accrue the unused leave credits of all permanent government personnel at year-end to ensure that the “Leave Benefits Payable” and the Government Equity accounts are reliable and leave benefits transactions are timely recorded in the correct period, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require all persons accountable to remit immediately the total amount reported under Cash in Local Treasury- Old account.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
A. Review the long outstanding checks, and require the Municipal Treasurer to cancel the unclaimed stale checks, and to furnish the Accounting Office with the original Disbursement Vouchers and its supporting documents, for the preparation of the corresponding Journal Entry Voucher pursuant to Section 59 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs; and
B.	Update the records to correctly reflect the cleared checks and stale checks, and ensure that all outstanding checks reflected in the Bank Reconciliation Statements are verified against the bank transaction records to ensure accuracy and reliability of the affected accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all purchases and issuances of Semi-Expendable properties are recorded in the appropriate Semi-Expendable Inventory and Expense accounts, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2024-006 and Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1; and
Require the GSO to issue Inventory Custodian Slips to the end-users of the semi-expendable properties to establish accountability over such items.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor undertake the following courses of action:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary journal entries to correct the balances of the “Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses” account and the affected permanent accounts; and
Advise the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all transactions are recorded in the correct accounts in the general ledger in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.

We recommended the following to the Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to trace and verify the cause of the variance in the intra-agency accounts, and effect the necessary adjustment; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to regularly reconcile the accounts, Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, to ensure correct account balances and present reliable data pursuant to Section 112 of Presidential Decree 1445 and Paragraph 27 of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards 1.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive, being charged with the primary responsibility of ensuring that all resources of the LGU are managed, expended or utilized in accordance with laws and regulations, and safeguarded against loss or wastage through illegal or improper disposition, ensure the following:

a.	Issue demand letters to the concerned accountable officers for the immediate remittance/deposit to the authorized government depository bank of the LGU the balance of Cash Local Treasury account amounting to P2,142,117.61;

b.	Exhaust all possible legal remedies to collect from the concerned accountable officers the total amount reported under Cash in Local Treasury account; and

c.	Submit to this Office the list of accountable officers with unremitted collections.
observations.managementAction: A total of P595,314.99 were remitted and deposited in CY 2024 to the LGU’s authorized government depository bank.
observations.reason: The Management has not exhausted all possible legal remedies to collect from the concerned accountable officers.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the reported balance of the Trust Liability-DRRM account and ensure that the proper accounting and reporting of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund pursuant to COA Circular 2012-002, Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 are strictly complied with.
observations.managementAction: The Management has complied with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Management:

a.	Optimally utilize the Municipality’s 20% Development Fund by ensuring that all PPAs under the said fund will be implemented timely in order to achieve the desired results intended for its beneficiaries;

b.	Prepare a comprehensive but realistic catch-up plan, after conducting proper monitoring and follow-up activities in order to expedite the project implementation of PPAs funded especially from prior years’ appropriation, which are still at the agency pipeline and to reduce backlogs; and

c.	Consider the need for realignment of the PPAs from prior years’ appropriations, where necessary, after determination of a more relevant and responsive and procurement-and-implementation-ready projects that may be included under the 20% Development Fund of the Municipality in adherence with applicable laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction:
The Management has complied with the audit recommendations.

The Municipal Development Council has convened regularly or as necessary to update the members of the status of implementation of its PPAs and to discuss appropriate actions to ensure the timely implementation of the said PPAs.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

a.	Carefully analyze and reconcile the entries made on the accounts with negative/abnormal balances; and 

b.	Prepare and submit the necessary adjusting entries to restore the correct balances of the assets and liability accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Management has reconciled the entries made and has prepared the necessary adjusting entries.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:

The Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, to prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers showing the names and the amounts due and collectible based on the updated Real Property Tax Account Register/ Taxpayer’s Index Card to provide an accurate and reliable basis in setting up the Receivables from RPT and SET; and

The Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher to record the RPT/SET Receivables established at the beginning of the year based on the submitted duly certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectible for the year and maintain the corresponding subsidiary ledgers to monitor tax collections and delinquencies.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented

Reiterated in Finding No. 7, Part II of this report.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant established the RPT/SET Receivables based on the Municipal Treasurer’s Certified List of Taxpayers. However, the Municipal Accountant did not maintain the subsidiary ledgers of the individual taxpayers to support the recorded balance. Moreover, the records of the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor are not reconciled.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, being charged with the primary responsibility of ensuring that all resources of the LGU are managed, expended or utilized in accordance with laws and regulations, and safeguarded against loss or wastage through illegal or improper disposition, ensure the following:
Issue demand letters to the concerned accountable officers for the immediate remittance/deposit to the authorized government depository bank of the LGU the balance of Cash Local Treasury account amounting to P2,142,117.61;
Exhaust all possible legal remedies to collect from the concerned accountable officers the total amount reported under Cash in Local Treasury account; and
Submit to this Office the list of accountable officers with unremitted collections.
We recommended that the Management, thru the Local Chief Executive, exhaust all possible remedies available to collect from the persons liable who are separated from the Municipality or deceased.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the reported balance of the Trust Liability-DRRM account and ensure that the proper accounting and reporting of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund pursuant to COA Circular 2012-002, Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 are strictly complied with.

We recommended that the Management:
Optimally utilize the Municipality’s 20% Development Fund by ensuring that all PPAs under the said fund will be implemented timely in order to achieve the desired results intended for its beneficiaries;
Prepare a comprehensive but realistic catch-up plan, after conducting proper monitoring and follow-up activities in order to expedite the project implementation of PPAs funded especially from prior years’ appropriation, which are still at the agency pipeline and to reduce backlogs; and
Consider the need for realignment of the PPAs from prior years’ appropriations, where necessary, after determination of a more relevant and responsive and procurement-and-implementation-ready projects that may be included under the 20% Development Fund of the Municipality in adherence with applicable laws, rules and regulations.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant:
Carefully analyze and reconcile the entries made on the accounts with negative/abnormal balances; and
Prepare and submit the necessary adjusting entries to restore the correct balances of the assets and liability accounts.

We recommended that Municipal Accountant set-up and maintain the subsidiary ledgers of the individual taxpayers to support the recorded balance of Real Property Tax (RPT) Receivable and Special Education Tax (SET) Receivable. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor ensure reconciliation of their respective balances to ascertain the accuracy, validity and reliability of the receivable accounts.

We recommended that the Accounting Office submit immediately the lacking disbursement vouchers, together with the supporting documents, and henceforth strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated 
September 15, 2009 on the submission of accounts and other reports of the Municipality on or before the set deadline, to avoid administrative sanctions and/or disallowance of said accounts, as provided for in Section 39 (1) & (3) of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit immediately to their authorized government depository bank the collections amounting to P2,942,122.77 as reported in the Financial Statements to avoid possible loss, misapplication or misappropriation of government funds and to impose administrative sanctions if warranted.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented

Reiterated in Finding No. 2, Part II of this report.

observations.reason: The list identifying the accountable officers with unremitted collections were not submitted by the Municipal Treasurer to the Office of the Municipal Mayor and to the Office of the Auditor. Consequently,  no demand letters were issued to the concerned accountable officers.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Management, thru the Local Chief Executive: 

Require the two (2) employees still connected with the Municipality to settle their excess claims for travelling expenses immediately; 

Exhaust all possible remedies available to collect from the persons liable who are separated or deceased, such as resorting to all legal remedies available to the LGU; and

Conduct regular monitoring and analysis of receivable accounts to ensure that these are collected when due and the immediate liquidation and settlement of all cash advances.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented

Reiterated in Finding No. 3, Part II of this report.

A total of P4,018,312.00, were settled in CY 2023.
observations.reason: The balance of the Other Receivables account amounting to P10,747,359.12 remained outstanding as at December 31, 2023

Partially Implemented

Necessary adjusting journal entries were taken up by the Accounting Section for properties that fall below the capitalization threshold of P15,000.00. Filed request for inspections for unserviceable properties.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Management, thru the Local Chief Executive, require the Inventory Committee to submit a duly accomplished RCPPE and all the necessary supporting documents pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to determine compliance of the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented

Management filed a request for inspection for properties considered to be unserviceable and a request for derecognition for missing and non-existing PPEs.
observations.reason: Procedures for disposal of unserviceable properties, dropping of non-existing and missing properties are still in process.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Management, thru the Local Chief Executive, undertake the following courses of action:

Require the Municipal Development Council to specify the details of the identified priority projects to be charged under the 20% Development Fund, including project location, specific implementation schedule, and physical target to avoid generic or lumpsum projects.

Stop the practice of implementing generic or lumpsum projects without further authority from the Sanggunian to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction:
Fully Implemented

The Management has complied with the audit  recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Management, through its MDRRMO, strictly comply with aforementioned regulations of COA Circular No. 2012-02 dated September 12, 2012 and NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) 
No. 2013-1 dated 
March 25, 2013 by ensuring that the LDRRMFIP shall include the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years.
observations.managementAction:
Fully Implemented

The Management has complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the MDRRM Officer to strive for the timely and full implementation of LDRRM related PPAs to achieve the goals and objective of the fund, as well as the desired benefits, in compliance with Republic Act No. 10121.
observations.managementAction:
Fully Implemented

The Management has complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive exhaust all remedies for the immediate deposit to their authorized depository bank the balance under 
Cash – Local Treasury as at December 31, 2021 amounting to P2,942,122.77 to avoid possible loss, misapplication or misappropriation of government funds.
observations.managementAction: The balance of Cash Local Treasury account remained undeposited.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 1 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Management, thru the Local Chief Executive:

a.	Direct all the accountable officers who are in custody of unserviceable properties to return the same to the GSO-Designate;

b.	Instruct the GSO-Designate to institute measures for the proper disposal of these properties in accordance with Title V of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992; and

c.	Instruct the Municipal Accountant to drop from the books the unserviceable properties only upon the completion of the disposal procedures using the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) provided by the COA to the Disposal Committee.
observations.managementAction: Unserviceable properties were still included in the PPE accounts of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 3 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Management, thru the Local Chief Executive, require the Accountable Officers of the lost watercrafts to immediately submit to this Office the duly endorsed Request for Relief from Property Accountability, together with the supporting documents and evidence as required in Section 151, Rule 22, Title V of the Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management in Local Governments. Otherwise, the accountable officers shall not be relieved of liability or allowed credit for any of such loss upon the settlement of his accounts.
observations.managementAction: These properties are still included in the PPE accounts of the Municipality. There was no Request for Relief of Property Accountability submitted to this Office as of year-end.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 3 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive, through its MDRRMO, strictly comply with aforementioned regulations of COA Circular No. 2012-02 dated September 12, 2012 and NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 by ensuring that the LDRRMFIP shall include the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years.
Further, we have recommended that the Management refrain from disbursing the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years without an approved budget as reflected in the AIP or LDRRMFIP.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMFIP for CY 2022 did not include the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 7 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit immediately to their authorized government depository bank the collections amounting to P2,942,122.77 as reported in the Financial Statements to avoid possible loss, misapplication or misappropriation of government funds and to impose administrative sanctions if warranted.

We recommended that the Management, thru the Local Chief Executive:
Require the two (2) employees still connected with the Municipality to settle their excess claims for travelling expenses immediately;
Exhaust all possible remedies available to collect from the persons liable who are separated or deceased, such as resorting to all legal remedies available to the LGU; and
Conduct regular monitoring and analysis of receivable accounts to ensure that these are collected when due and the immediate liquidation and settlement of all cash advances.

We recommended that the Management, thru the Local Chief Executive, require the Inventory Committee to submit a duly accomplished RCPPE and all the necessary supporting documents pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to determine compliance of the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Management, thru the Local Chief Executive, undertake the following courses of action:
Require the Municipal Development Council to specify the details of the identified priority projects to be charged under the 20% Development Fund, including project location, specific implementation schedule, and physical target to avoid generic or lumpsum projects.
b. Stop the practice of implementing generic or lumpsum projects without further authority from the Sanggunian to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
The Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, to prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers showing the names and the amounts due and collectible based on the updated Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s Index Card to provide an accurate and reliable basis in setting up the Receivables from RPT and SET; and
The Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher to record the RPT/SET Receivables established at the beginning of the year based on the submitted duly certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectible for the year and maintain the corresponding subsidiary ledgers to monitor tax collections and delinquencies.

We recommended that the Management, through its MDRRMO, strictly comply with aforementioned regulations of COA Circular No. 2012-02 dated September 12, 2012 and NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 by ensuring that the LDRRMFIP shall include the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years.

Further, we recommended that the Management refrain from disbursing the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years without the proper approval of the revised AIP or LDRRMFIP from the Local Sanggunian and the LCE.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the MDRRM Officer to strive for the timely and full implementation of LDRRM related PPAs to achieve the goals and objective of the fund, as well as the desired benefits, in compliance with Republic Act No. 10121.

We have recommended that the Management ensure that payments for the SRA are supported with relevant and pertinent documents pursuant to Section 4 of PD 1445 to establish compliance with the conditions set under AO No. 36 dated November 16, 2020 and DBM – DOH Joint Circular No. 2, s. 2020 dated November 25, 2020.
observations.managementAction: The Management has submitted the relevant supporting documents to support the payment of COVID-19 SRA.
The Disbursing Officer has liquidated her outstanding cash advances.

The receivables from the Livelihood Program of the LGU has been settled. A total of P4,012,000.00, which pertains to the accountability of the former Municipal Treasurer and other employees of the LGU were settled prior to the publication of the Report.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 2 of this report.

observations.managementAction: The Management conducted a physical inventory of all its PPE relative to the one-time cleansing of the said account. However, it was observed that the submitted RPCPPE lacks relevant information, which casts doubt on the reliability and completeness of the procedures undertaken by the Municipality.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding Nos. 3 and 4 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Management refrain from charging salaries and wages of job order personnel under the 20% Development Fund and strictly adhere with the pertinent provisions of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020 and DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-a dated February 22, 2017 in the utilization of the said Fund.
observations.managementAction: Salaries and wages of job order personnel were no longer charged under the 20% Development Fund starting the 2nd Quarter of CY 2022.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Management submit the lacking supporting documents to avoid suspension in audit. Likewise, we have also required that Management submit the complete supporting documents as required by COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 in the payment of government transactions.
observations.managementAction: The Management has submitted the lacking supporting documents of the various expenses charged under the LDRRMF of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Management secure the proper permits and licenses of its radio communication equipment and radio station from the NTC to avoid possible fines and penalties arising from non-compliance pursuant to RA 3846 or the Radio Laws of the Philippines.
observations.managementAction: The Management has secured the necessary permits and licenses of its radio communication equipment and radio station from the NTC.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management optimally utilize the municipality’s 20% Development Fund by ensuring that all programs, projects and activities under the said fund will be implemented timely in order to achieve the desired welfare intended for its beneficiaries.
Likewise, we have also recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Local Development Council to conduct feasibility study of all projects included in the 20% Development Fund and ensure that each project to be included is carefully planned in order to avoid delay in the implementation.  Moreover, we have recommended that the Management submit the status of implementation for funds, which were transferred to various barangays.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has optimally utilized its 20% Development Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the following:

a.	End-User Units to prepare the PPMPs in accordance with Section 7 of RA 9184 using the format prescribed by the GPPB (Annex A) and submit the same to the Municipal Budget Officer for evaluation and consideration in the budget proposal;

b.	BAC, through the BAC Secretariat, to consolidate the PPMPs and prepare the APP in accordance with the applicable provisions of RA 9184 using the prescribed format under GPPB Circular No. 07-2015 dated July 16, 2015. (Note: e-copy of the prescribed APP can be downloaded at gppb.gov.ph/downloadables)

c.	Municipal Accountant to check thoroughly the completeness of supporting documents before processing and recommending approval of payment for any procurement.
observations.managementAction: The Management has prepared the APP and PPMP for CY 2022 using the prescribed format in compliance with RA No. 9184.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 4 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994 and Section 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-01 dated June 14, 2012 on the submission, and liquidation of fund transfer received from National Government Agencies (NGAs).  The Municipal Accountant is advised to collate records to ensure that all unspent balances of fund transfers for completed projects irrespective of source agency be returned.
observations.managementAction: The Management has liquidated the fund transfers received from NGAs.
The balance of Cash Local Treasury account remained undeposited.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Finding No. 2
of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

1.	Enforce payment or cause the deduction from their salaries or any amount due to employees who are still connected with the municipality whose disallowances remain unsettled and submit to this Office proof of payment made by the persons liable;     

2.	Exhaust all remedies to collect from the persons liable who are no longer connected with the municipality, otherwise, determine other persons jointly and severally liable in lieu of these persons and demand payment thereof; and

3.	Strictly monitor and enforce settlement of disallowances in accordance with the procedures under the RRSA prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Finding No. 4
of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management ensure that payments for the SRA are supported with relevant and pertinent documents pursuant to Section 4 of PD 1445 to establish compliance with the conditions set under AO No. 36 dated November 16, 2020 and DBM – DOH Joint Circular No. 2, s. 2020 dated November 25, 2020.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive exhaust all remedies for the immediate deposit to their authorized depository bank the balance under 
Cash – Local Treasury as at December 31, 2021 amounting to P2,942,122.77 to avoid possible loss, misapplication or misappropriation of government funds.

We recommended that the Management, thru the Local Chief Executive:
Direct all the accountable officers who are in custody of unserviceable properties to return the same to the GSO-Designate;
Instruct the GSO-Designate to institute measures for the proper disposal of these properties in accordance with Title V of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to drop from the books the unserviceable properties only upon the completion of the disposal procedures using the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) provided by the COA to the Disposal Committee.

We recommended that the Management, thru the Local Chief Executive, require the Accountable Officers of the lost watercrafts to immediately submit to this Office the duly endorsed Request for Relief from Property Accountability, together with the supporting documents and evidence as required in Section 151, Rule 22, Title V of the Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management in Local Governments. Otherwise, the accountable officers shall not be relieved of liability or allowed credit for any of such loss upon the settlement of his accounts.

We recommended that the Management refrain from charging salaries and wages of job order personnel under the 20% Development Fund and strictly adhere with the pertinent provisions of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020 and DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-a dated February 22, 2017 in the utilization of the said Fund.

We recommended that the Management submit the lacking supporting documents to avoid suspension in audit. Likewise, we also required that Management submit the complete supporting documents as required by COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 in the payment of government transactions.

We recommended that the Management secure the proper permits and licenses of its radio communication equipment and radio station from the NTC to avoid possible fines and penalties arising from non-compliance pursuant to RA 3846 or the Radio Laws of the Philippines.

We recommended that management require the Inventory Committee the immediate submission of the original copy of the RCPPE of the municipality for CY 2020 and observe the timely submission of the said report on or before January 31 of the following year to give ample time for the Audit Team for its review and verify compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to ensure that a reliable amount of PPE account is presented in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: An RPCPPE was submitted but the conduct of annual physical count was not in accordance to COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Finding No. 5
of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer reconcile her cashbook balances regularly with the Accounting Office as provided for by Book 2 Chapter 2 Section 63 of Local Treasury Operation Manual of Bureau of Local Government Finance for a strong internal control system of the municipality to bolster the cash account of the Municipality. Moreover, the Municipal Treasurer should give her written explanation for the non-reconciliation of cashbooks balances.
observations.managementAction: The cashbook balance of the Municipal Treasurer is reconciled on a monthly basis.

The Local Chief Executive issued Municipal Memorandum No. 1 dated March 8, 2021, effective on the same date, requiring the Municipal Treasurer, all collectors, liquidating officers and cashiers to observe the policies and procedures on the daily turnover of collections, verification of collections and accountable forms, deposit of collections, safeguarding of collections and frequency of deposit. The said Circular also provides for the administrative sanctions for violation thereof.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

1.	Direct all concerned departments who are receiving goods in-kind from National Government Agencies or from any source, to inform and coordinate with the General Services Office (GSO), for the inspection and receipt of such goods, before issuance to the concerned end users or recipients; 

2.	Instruct the DRRM Officer to submit the Summary/List of Donation Received, Distributed and Balances, and the Inventory of Undistributed Items at the end of each quarter to NDRRMC, through the OCD, copy furnished the Auditor, as mandated under COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 to attain transparency and accountability for all in-kind donations thereby ensuring that the donations have reached the intended recipients; and

3.	Instruct the Municipal Accountant to reflect the disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements pertinent to the receipt, distribution and undistributed balances as at year-end of all donations in-kind received by the Municipality as required in Section VII (2) of COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014.
observations.managementAction: A report was prepared and submitted by the Management on the donations in-kind received.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management, thru the Municipal Accountant, to comply strictly with the provisions of Chapter 7 of the Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for Local Government Units (LGUs) and ensure that all cost attributable to the repair/improvement of assets are capitalized accordingly. Further, we recommend the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to restore the correct balances of the assets and equity accounts.
observations.managementAction: Journal Entry Voucher No. 100-21-03-10 dated March 31, 2021 to correct the balances of the affected accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to post all the required information in the GPPB Online Portal relating to emergency procurement and comply with all other requirements to ensure transparency and accountability in the implementation of the Bayanihan Act in government procurement.
observations.managementAction: The Management has complied with the posting requirements in the GPPB Online Portal.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management optimally utilize the municipality’s 20% Development Fund by ensuring that all programs, projects and activities under the said fund will be implemented timely in order to achieve the desired welfare intended for its beneficiaries.
Likewise, we also recommended that the Local Chief Executive require Local Development Council to conduct feasibility study of all projects included in the 20% Development Fund and ensure that each project to be included are carefully planned in order to avoid delay in the implementation.  Moreover, we recommend management to submit the status of implementation for funds which were transferred to various barangays.
observations.managementAction: There are still some projects which remained unimplemented at year end.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive through its MDRRMO strictly comply with aforementioned regulations of COA Circular No. 2012-02 dated September 12, 2012 and NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 by ensuring that the LDRRMFIP shall include the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years.
Further, we recommended management to refrain from disbursing the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years without an approved budget as reflected in the AIP or LDRRMFIP.
observations.managementAction: On-going implementation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive to require the following:

a.	End-User Units to prepare the PPMPs in accordance with Section 7 of RA 9184 using the format prescribed by the GPPB (Annex A) and submit the same to the Municipal Budget Officer for evaluation and consideration in the budget proposal;
b.	BAC through the BAC Secretariat to consolidate the PPMPs and prepare the APP in accordance with the applicable provisions of RA 9184 using the prescribed format under GPPB Circular No. 07-2015 dated July 16, 2015. (Note: e-copy of the prescribed APP can be downloaded at gppb.gov.ph/downloadables)
c.	Municipal Accountant to check thoroughly the completeness of supporting documents before processing and recommending approval of payment for any procurement.
observations.managementAction: On-going implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 4 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994 and Section 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-01 dated June 14, 2012 on the submission, and liquidation of fund transfer received from National Government Agencies (NGAs).  The Municipal Accountant is advised to collate records to ensure that all unspent balances of fund transfers for completed projects irrespective of source agency be returned.
observations.managementAction: Some projects funded by NGAs are still being implemented by the LGU.

observations.reason:
Reiterated in Finding No. 1
of this report.

Reiterated in Finding No. 1
of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the appropriate adjusting journal entries to correct the misstatements of the affected accounts. Henceforth, ensure that costs of major repairs, rehabilitation, improvement and construction of infrastructure assets should be capitalized or recognized as property, plant and equipment in accordance with Philippines Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) No. 17.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 7 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management optimally utilize the municipality’s 20% Development Fund by ensuring that all programs, projects and activities under the said fund will be implemented timely in order to achieve the desired welfare intended for its beneficiaries.
Likewise, we also recommended that the Local Chief Executive require Local Development Council to conduct feasibility study of all projects included in the 20% Development Fund and ensure that each project to be included are carefully planned in order to avoid delay in the implementation.  Moreover, we recommend management to submit the status of implementation for funds which were transferred to various barangays.

We recommended that management require the Inventory Committee the immediate submission of the original copy of the RCPPE of the municipality for CY 2020 and observe the timely submission of the said report on or before January 31 of the following year to give ample time for the Audit Team for its review and verify compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to ensure that a reliable amount of PPE account is presented in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer reconcile her cashbook balances regularly with the Accounting Office as provided for by Book 2 Chapter 2 Section 63 of Local Treasury Operation Manual of Bureau of Local Government Finance for a strong internal control system of the municipality to bolster the cash account of the Municipality. Moreover, the Municipal Treasurer should give her written explanation for the non-reconciliation of cashbooks balances.

We recommended that management:
Enforce payment or cause the deduction from their salaries or any amount due to employees who are still connected with the municipality whose disallowances remain unsettled and submit to this Office proof of payment made by the persons liable;
Exhaust all remedies to collect from the persons liable who are no longer connected with the municipality, otherwise, determine other persons jointly and severally liable in lieu of these persons and demand payment thereof; and
Strictly monitor and enforce settlement of disallowances in accordance with the procedures under the RRSA prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that management:
Direct all concerned departments who are receiving goods in-kind from National Government Agencies or from any source, to inform and coordinate with the General Services Office (GSO), for the inspection and receipt of such goods, before issuance to the concerned end users or recipients;
Instruct the DRRM Officer to submit the Summary/List of Donation Received, Distributed and Balances, and the Inventory of Undistributed Items at the end of each quarter to NDRRMC, through the OCD, copy furnished the Auditor, as mandated under COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 to attain transparency and accountability for all in-kind donations thereby ensuring that the donations have reached the intended recipients; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to reflect the disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements pertinent to the receipt, distribution and undistributed balances as at year-end of all donations in-kind received by the Municipality as required in Section VII (2) of COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014.

We recommended Management, thru the Municipal Accountant, to comply strictly with the provisions of Chapter 7 of the Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for Local Government Units (LGUs) and ensure that all cost attributable to the repair/improvement of assets are capitalized accordingly. Further, we recommend the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to restore the correct balances of the assets and equity accounts.

We recommended management to post all the required information in the GPPB Online Portal relating to emergency procurement and comply with all other requirements to ensure transparency and accountability in the implementation of the Bayanihan Act in government procurement.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to compute the depreciation expense, prepare the lapsing schedule and draw necessary journal entry to take up depreciation expense for the current year, update the related accumulated depreciation and record adjustment in government equity account for unrecorded prior years’ depreciation expense to present fairly the financial statements in accordance with PPSAS 17 and pertinent provisions of Item IV of  COA Circular No.2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting Office computed for the depreciation expense, prepared lapsing schedule and draw the necessary journal entry to take up the depreciation expense.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive require the Acting Municipal Treasurer and the concerned collectors the immediate deposit with the Municipality’s authorized depository bank the balance under Cash – Local Treasury (old) as at December 31, 2019 amounting to P3,501,545.97 to avoid possible loss, misapplication or misappropriation of government funds and disciplinary action.
observations.managementAction: A collection amounting to P546,763.20 was made and deposited during the year.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Finding No. 2
of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management, thru the Municipal Accountant, comply strictly with the provisions of Section 111 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2010-001 by ensuring that all accounts presented in the financial statements are supported with necessary subsidiary schedules and submit the same to COA in order to furnish the necessary information relevant to audit and to ensure the validity of the account balances presented in the financial statements. 
Further, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant exhaust all possible means to trace and support the reported balance of the “Trust Liabilities” account and prepare the necessary correcting journal entries in order to reflect a more reliable financial information. Likewise, we require the submission of the Journal Entry Vouchers together with the supporting documents relative to the correction that has to be made.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require, by sending demand letter, the concerned Accountable Officer to liquidate immediately her outstanding cash advances. Refrain from granting additional cash advances to the said AO and observe strictly the rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
Likewise, management should enforce sanctions under COA Circular No. 97-002 for the non-liquidation of cash advances which includes the refund of cash advance through salary deductions as provided under Item 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002, where necessary.
observations.managementAction: The cash advance was fully liquidated as of year end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management realign the fund of the projects which have not yet started in order to comply with the aforementioned regulations and henceforth strictly comply with the provisions of DILG DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 with regard to the appropriation and utilization of 20% DF that would focus on the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcome.
observations.managementAction: The municipality passed SB Resolution No. 51 series of 2020 realigning, among others, P7,570,500.00 from the 20% Development Fund to fund projects, programs and activities of the LGU to boost their capacity in fighting COVID-19.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management comply with the provisions of IRR of RA 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and submit the Report on the Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF using the format in Annex B on or before the 15th day after the end of each month as prescribed in Sec. 5.1.5 of the same Circular to facilitate compliance of the Municipality on the proper accounting and reporting of the fund.

b.	BAC through the BAC Secretariat to consolidate the PPMPs and prepare the APP in accordance with the applicable provisions of RA 9184 using the prescribed format under GPPB Circular No. 07-2015 dated July 16, 2015. (Note: e-copy of the prescribed APP can be downloaded at gppb.gov.ph/downloadables)
c.	Municipal Accountant to check thoroughly the completeness of supporting documents before processing and recommending approval of payment for any procurement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile its record with DOH RO 2 and ensure the timely submission of the required liquidation reports to DOH RO 2 to settle this liability pursuant to Section 111 Presidential Decree No. 1445.

The Municipal Accountant is advised to collate records to ensure that all unspent balances of fund transfers for completed projects irrespective of source agency be returned.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend the Local Chief Executive require the previous Municipal Treasurer the immediate deposit with the Municipality’s authorized depository bank the balance under Cash – Local Treasury (old) as at December 31, 2018 amounting to P3,580,202.82 to avoid possible loss, misapplication or misappropriation of government funds. 
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the transactions recorded in the Cash-Local Treasury (new) account in order to determine the cause of abnormal balance and initiate corrections or adjustments to come up with the actual balance of the said account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 4 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994 and Section 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-01 dated June 14, 2012 on the submission, and liquidation of fund transfer received from National Government Agencies (NGAs).  The Municipal Accountant is advised to collate records to ensure that all unspent balances of fund transfers for completed projects irrespective of source agency be returned.

We recommended that the management:
1.	Adhere to the provision of Section 5 DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 to ensure the optimal utilization of 20% development fund to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the municipality.
2.	Submit justifications for the delay and non-implementation/completion of the above-mentioned priority projects totalling P12.74 million and P6.3 million in CY 2018 and CY 2017, respectively.

3.	Initiate corrective measures to fast-track the implementation and completion of unimplemented and ongoing priority projects for CY 2017 and 2018 in order to avoid accumulation of unimplemented projects and to ensure the optimal utilization of 20% development fund in the ensuing year at lower cost due to soaring prices of commodities.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 9 of this report.
