We recommended that upon the completion of physical count of the Inventory Committee, the Local Chief Executive require the GSO Designate to institute measures to properly dispose all unserviceable properties pursuant to Section 79 of PD 1445 and in accordance with the procedures cited in Title V of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive require the Acting Municipal Treasurer and the concerned collectors the immediate deposit with the Municipality’s authorized depository bank the balance under Cash – Local Treasury (old) as at December 31, 2019 amounting to P3,501,545.97 to avoid possible loss, misapplication or misappropriation of government funds and disciplinary action.

We recommend that Management, thru the Municipal Accountant, comply strictly with the provisions of Section 111 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2010-001 by ensuring that all accounts presented in the financial statements are supported with necessary subsidiary schedules and submit the same to COA in order to furnish the necessary information relevant to audit and to ensure the validity of the account balances presented in the financial statements.
Further, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant exhaust all possible means to trace and support the reported balance of the “Trust Liabilities” account and prepare the necessary correcting journal entries in order to reflect a more reliable financial information. Likewise, we require the submission of the Journal Entry Vouchers together with the supporting documents relative to the correction that has to be made.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the appropriate adjusting journal entries to correct the misstatements of the affected accounts. Henceforth, ensure that costs of major repairs, rehabilitation, improvement and construction of infrastructure assets should be capitalized or recognized as property, plant and equipment in accordance with Philippines Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) No. 17.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require, by sending demand letter, the concerned Accountable Officer to liquidate immediately her outstanding cash advances. Refrain from granting additional cash advances to the said AO and observe strictly the rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
Likewise, management should enforce sanctions under COA Circular 
No. 97-002 for the non-liquidation of cash advances which includes the refund of cash advance through salary deductions as provided under Item 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002, where necessary.

We recommended that management optimally utilize the municipality’s 20% Development Fund by ensuring that all programs, projects and activities under the said fund will be implemented timely in order to achieve the desired welfare intended for its beneficiaries.
Likewise, we also recommended that the Local Chief Executive require Local Development Council to conduct feasibility study of all projects included in the 20% Development Fund and ensure that each project to be included are carefully planned in order to avoid delay in the implementation.  Moreover, we recommend management to submit the status of implementation for funds which were transferred to various barangays.

We recommended that the Management realign the fund of the projects which have not yet started in order to comply with the aforementioned regulations and henceforth strictly comply with the provisions of DILG DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 with regard to the appropriation and utilization of 20% DF that would focus on the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcome.

We recommended Management comply with the provisions of IRR of RA 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and submit the Report on the Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF using the format in Annex B on or before the 15th day after the end of each month as prescribed in Sec. 5.1.5 of the same Circular to facilitate compliance of the Municipality on the proper accounting and reporting of the fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive through its MDRRMO strictly comply with aforementioned regulations of COA Circular No. 2012-02 dated September 12, 2012 and NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 by ensuring that the LDRRMFIP shall include the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years.
Further, we recommended management to refrain from disbursing the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years without an approved budget as reflected in the AIP or LDRRMFIP.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to require the following:
End-User Units to prepare the PPMPs in accordance with Section 7 of RA 9184 using the format prescribed by the GPPB (Annex A) and submit the same to the Municipal Budget Officer for evaluation and consideration in the budget proposal;
BAC through the BAC Secretariat to consolidate the PPMPs and prepare the APP in accordance with the applicable provisions of RA 9184 using the prescribed format under GPPB Circular No. 07-2015 dated July 16, 2015. (Note: e-copy of the prescribed APP can be downloaded at gppb.gov.ph/downloadables)
Municipal Accountant to check thoroughly the completeness of supporting documents before processing and recommending approval of payment for any procurement.

We recommended that that Acting Municipal Treasurer and Disbursing Officers maintain Cashbooks per fund and record their daily transactions in the corresponding books. Also, Collectors should maintain cashbook to record cash collections and remittances made to establish an internal control system on the collection of revenues and disbursement of funds of the municipality thereby safeguarding government funds in the custody of the different accountable officers.

We recommended that Management designate the accountable officers either as Collecting Officers or Disbursing/Special Disbursing Officers solely, as appropriate, in order to have a sound internal control system pursuant to the provisions of Sections 123 and 124 of PD 1445.

We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the land and other properties held for rent recorded under Land, Market and Hostels and Dormitories accounts to Investment Property-Land and Building accounts to conform with 
IPSAS 16-Investment Property for fair presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile its record with DOH RO 2 and ensure the timely submission of the required liquidation reports to DOH RO 2 to settle this liability pursuant to Section 111 Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We recommended that management fill up the position for LDRRM Officer as required by Section 12 (b) of RA 10121 and Sections 4.2 and 5.3 of NDRRMC, DILG, DBM and CSC JMC No. 2014-1 to efficiently and effectively discharge the LDRRM functions of the municipality to ensure the full attainment of the objectives of the appropriated fund to address the risks and effects of disaster affecting the locality. Likewise, the Office of the Sanggunian Bayan is enjoined to create the three (3) statutory DRRM staff positions of the LDRRM Office in accordance with the aforementioned law and circular.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the unexpended balance of the DRRMF-CO and continuing appropriation to the General Fund pursuant to Section 5.1.11 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that Management through the Local Solid Waste Management Board prepare the required 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan and submit the same for approval of the National Solid Waste Management Commission in compliance with Section 16 of RA 9003 as basis for comprehensive action to address the solid waste management concerns of the municipality for a better protection of public health and environment in the community and for the local government officials to avoid administrative sanctions provided for by the Act.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct annual physical inventory of the properties of the municipality and to prepare a report thereon to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation with that of the accounting records, copy furnished the municipal auditor, pursuant to the provisions of Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.
observations.managementAction: The management committed to implement the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: This was discussed during the Exit Conference on April 16, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Local Chief Executive require the concerned accountable officer/s the immediate deposit with its authorized depository bank the balance under Cash – Local Treasury (old) as at December 31, 2017 amounting to P3,731,867.16 to avoid possible loss, misapplication or misappropriation of government funds.
observations.managementAction: Management explained that substantial portion of these collections pertains to the balances before the turnover of accountabilities between the outgoing Municipal Treasurer and incoming Assistant Municipal Treasurer. They committed to monitor and demand the deposit of these collections.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 2 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Solid Waste Management Plan be duly approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission to ensure that the programs and activities included in such plan are consistent with the National Solid Waste Management Framework and to ensure that the municipality adopted a systematic, comprehensive, and ecological waste management program.
observations.managementAction: The preparation of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan (ESWMP) of the municipality is on-going. They committed to submit the plan to the NSWC not later than April 30, 2019.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 10 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management optimally utilize its 20% Development Fund by ensuring that all planned programs, projects and activities under the said fund will be implemented timely in order to achieve the desired benefit intended for its beneficiaries.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 11 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality carefully plan the implementation of projects funded by other agencies to effect the timely delivery of basic services to the people most especially the intended beneficiaries of these programs.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 7 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2002-01 dated June 14, 2012.  Management is advised to submit and liquidate all fund transfer upon completion of the projects/programs.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 7 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
2.9	We recommended the Local Chief Executive require the previous Municipal Treasurer the immediate deposit with the Municipality’s authorized depository bank the balance under Cash – Local Treasury (old) as at December 31, 2018 amounting to P3,580,202.82 to avoid possible loss, misapplication or misappropriation of government funds.
2.10 Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the transactions recorded in the Cash-Local Treasury (new) account in order to determine the cause of abnormal balance and initiate corrections or adjustments to come up with the actual balance of the said account.

3.4	We recommended that Acting Municipal Treasurer and Disbursing Officers maintain Cashbooks per fund and record their daily transactions in the corresponding books. Also, Collectors should maintain cashbook to record cash collections and remittances made to establish an internal control system on the collection of revenues and disbursement of funds of the municipality thereby safeguarding government funds in the custody of the different accountable officers.

4.8	We recommended that Management designate the accountable officers either as Collecting Officers or Disbursing/Special Disbursing Officers solely, as appropriate, in order to have a sound internal control system pursuant to the provisions of Sections 123 and 124 of PD 1445.
4.9	Likewise, the accountable officers and the Management review the fidelity bond filed and allocate sufficient funds to ensure that the accountable officer is appropriately and adequately bonded as to his/her function in compliance with Section 4.2 of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009.

5.5	We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the land and other properties held for rent recorded under Land, Market and Hostels and Dormitories accounts to Investment Property-Land and Building accounts to conform with 
IPSAS 16-Investment Property for fair presentation of the financial statements.

6.4	We recommend that the Municipal Accountant reconcile its record with DOH RO 2 and ensure the timely submission of the required liquidation reports to DOH RO 2 to settle this liability pursuant to Section 111 Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We recommended that management follow strictly the provisions of Section 96 of RA 10717 for the timely review, monitoring and evaluation of management’s action on previous year’s audit recommendations.

We recommended that the Accounting Office strictly adhere with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 on the submission of accounts and other reports of the municipality on or before the set deadline to facilitate timely review and evaluation of transactions for early detection of errors and deficiencies. Further, we require the Accounting Office the immediate submission of the disbursement vouchers for the period October 1 to December 31, 2017.

We recommended that Management establish a Local Council for the Protection of Children (LCPC) to coordinate with and assist the LGU for the adoption of a comprehensive plan on delinquency prevention, and to oversee its proper implementation. Also, direct the Municipal Budget Officer to provide the necessary appropriation representing one percent (1%) of the IRA for the strengthening and implementation of the programs of the LCPC.
We recommended that Management comply with the provisions of PCW-DILG-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2013-01 dated July 18, 2013 on the preparation, review and approval of GAD Plan and Budget.

We recommended that Management through the Local School Board prepare its Annual Budget of Special Education Fund in accordance with Section 100 and 272 of the Local Government Code by indicating the priority education programs/projects/activities to ensure that the SEF will be spent for the purpose for which it was intended and to include programs, projects and activities for Early Childhood Care and Development (ECCD) in adherence to Section 7(b) of Republic Act No. 10410.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the minimum net take home pay set in the GAA for a given year. It must also ensure that the net take home pays of the employees for the ensuing year will not fall below P5,000.00 pursuant to Section 48 of RA 10964 or the General Appropriations Act for Fiscal Year 2018.  Furthermore, we recommend management to avoid allowing salary deductions arising from loans if such deductions will lower his net take home pay beyond the threshold.

We recommended that management closely monitor the LDRRM Plan to ensure that programs/activities are timely implemented in addressing projected climate risk and enhancing disaster capabilities at LGU level. Further, we recommend management, in coordination with the MDRRM Council, to formulate a Comprehensive and Integrated MDRRM Plan.
observations.managementAction: The management explained that the delays in the implementation of their projects is due to bad weather conditions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the concerned officer to submit Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the prescribed format, on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the Office of the Auditor.. Further, we recommend management to comply with the provision of Sections 5.1.2 and 5.1.15 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 by conducting a regular review and update of its LDRRMFIP and submit the same to Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that all undeposited collections totalling P4,255,888.87 as of December 31, 2016 should be deposited intact with its authorized depository bank pursuant to the aforementioned regulation and to avoid exposing the funds to the risk of possible loss, misapplication or misappropriation.
observations.managementAction: Management explained that substantial portion of these collections pertains to the balances before the turnover of accountabilities between the outgoing Municipal Treasurer and incoming Assistant Municipal Treasurer. Cash examination was conducted by the Provincial Treasurer’s Office (PTO) to determine the extent of accountability of all collectors and the Assistant Municipal Treasurer.  The accountability of the present Assistant Municipal Treasurer after the turnover was fully established.  Notwithstanding the result of the cash examination conducted by the PTO, management will continue to monitor and demand the deposit of these collections.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 2 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to comply with the provisions of PCW-DILG-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2013-01 dated July 18, 2013 on the preparation, review and approval of GAD Plan and Budget.  The GAD Focal Person is advised to monitor and evaluate the implementation of programs and activities of the approved GPB.
observations.managementAction: Management explained that they have submitted their GAD Plan and Budget for CY 2016 to the Provincial Planning and Development Office (PPDO) Cagayan who submitted a copy to the DILG Cagayan Province Office.  However, the municipality has not received any communication/feedback on the submitted GAD Plan and Budget. They explained that the formulated programs and activities in their GAD Plan were based on the gender issues and concerns identified in the municipality. Although, they were not certain whether these programs and activities are gender responsive in accordance GAD related laws. Management emphasized the importance of DILG’s mandate to review the annual GAD Plans, Programs and Budgets and its endorsement for integration in the Annual Budget and Annual Investment Plan (AIP).
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 6 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive create a committee to conduct physical inventory of the properties of the municipality and to prepare a report thereon to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation with that of the accounting records, pursuant to the provisions of Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.

The Sanggunian Bayan Members should facilitate the creation of position for General Services Officer (GSO) who will take custody of and accountable for all properties owned by the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to implement the audit recommendation. They also committed to propose to the Sanggunian Bayan the creation of permanent/regular position for General Services Officer (GSO).
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 1 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to management to fast tract the completion of all on–going and not implemented projects funded projects under the 20% Development Fund to optimize utilization thereof and timely brings the desired benefits to the intended beneficiaries.  Likewise, the concerned department is advised to monitor regularly the status of implementation of the 20% Development Plan to achieve the desired goals and benefits.
observations.managementAction: Management explained that the appropriated amount for the Core Shelter Project 1 was the counterpart of the LGU in anticipation for Financial Assistance from Department of Social Welfare and Development (DSWD). However, no funds was transferred by the DSWD, hence, the municipality decided to implement the project at their own level as of year-end.  The amount appropriated for the Construction of Multi-purpose Buildings and procurement of post-harvest facilities were not implemented due to bad weather condition (typhoons) on the latter period of the year. Other funds appropriated were LGU counterparts in anticipation of any financial assistance from various National Agencies.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 10 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct annual physical inventory of the properties of the municipality and to prepare a report thereon to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation with that of the accounting records, copy furnished the municipal auditor, pursuant to the provisions of Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.

We recommend the Local Chief Executive require the concerned accountable officer/s the immediate deposit with its authorized depository bank the balance under Cash – Local Treasury (old) as at December 31, 2017 amounting to P3,731,867.16 to avoid possible loss, misapplication or misappropriation of government funds.

We recommended to management and to the Sanggunian Bayan Members to facilitate the creation of position for LDRRM Officer or its equivalent in accordance to Section 4.2 of JMC No. 2014-01 (NDRRMC-DILG_DBM-CSC) dated April 4, 2014.  Management should revisit the LDRRMP and ensure that performance indicators and measures are specific, measurable, achievable relevant and time bound to facilitate assessment of the progress toward the achievement of the outcomes, objectives and goals.  Moreover, the approved LDRRMP as well as the LDRRMFIP are submitted to the OCD for review and evaluation.
observations.managementAction: The management agreed to implement the audit recommendation. They explained that a proposal has been made to the Sanggunian Bayan for the creation of a permanent/regular position of a LDRRM Officer. They agreed to implement our audit recommendations through the LDRRM Office that shall be responsible for setting the direction, development, implementation, and coordination of DRRM programs and shall perform research and planning, administrative and training, operations and warning and those functions as may be authorized by the LDRRMC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly comply with the submission/transmittal of executed contracts together with supporting documents within five (5) days after its perfection to afford the audit team a prompt review and evaluation thereof.  Any defects/deficiency in result of the review shall be timely communicated to management as a tool to adopt corrective measures and/or improve their operational and procurement processes.
observations.managementAction: Management admitted the delay in the submission of perfected contracts with its supporting documents due to unforeseen weather conditions, however, they agreed to follow the rules set under Section 1.1 of COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Accountant ensures that the supporting documents are complete before effecting payment to prove the validity, propriety and legality of the claim pursuant to existing law, rules and regulations. Moreover, management is advised to submit the lacking documents and comply with the other deficiencies noted to avoid audit suspension/disallowance of the transactions.
observations.managementAction:
Management agreed with the audit recommendation.
The Municipal Accountant committed to submit the lacking documents and to comply with the other noted deficiencies in the audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Accountant ensures that the supporting documents of the disbursement vouchers are complete before effecting payment to prove the regularity and validity of the claim pursuant to existing law, rules and regulations. Moreover, management is advised to submit the lacking documents and comply with the other deficiencies noted to avoid audit suspension/disallowance of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed with the audit recommendations. The Municipal Accountant committed to submit the lacking documents and comply with other noted deficiencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2002-01 dated June 14, 2012.  Management is advised to submit and liquidate all fund transfer upon completion of the projects/programs.
observations.managementAction: Management explained that some of these programs were fully implemented, while other programs are on-going. They committed to submit and liquidate all fund transfer upon completion of the projects/programs..

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management thru the MSWMB, make representation on the approval of their ESWMP and execute the programs and activities contained therein.  Regular monitoring and review of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan should be conducted, and if warranted, make corrective action plan to address the present issues and concerns on ecological environment.
observations.managementAction: Management explained that they have already identified a site for their sanitary landfill subject to the approval of the Mines and Geosciences Bureau (MGB). They also committed to make representations on the approval of their ESWMP which was already submitted to the Department of Environment and Natural Resources (DENR).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that all collections of the municipality should be deposited intact regularly with its authorized depository bank pursuant to the aforementioned regulation and to avoid exposing the funds to the risk of possible loss, misapplication or misappropriation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer does not regularly deposit her cash collections to the municipality’s authorized depository bank.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 1 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to create a committee to conduct physical inventory of the properties of the municipality and to prepare a report thereon to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation with that of the accounting records, pursuant to the provisions of Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.
observations.managementAction: Management have not conducted physical inventory of its property, plant and equipment.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 8 of the report.

observations.recommendation: 1.5 	We recommended that all undeposited collections totalling P4,255,888.87 as of December 31, 2016 should be deposited intact with its authorized depository bank pursuant to the aforementioned regulation and to avoid exposing the funds to the risk of possible loss, misapplication or misappropriation.

2.5 	We recommended management to comply with the provisions of PCW-DILG-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2013-01 dated July 18, 2013 on the preparation, review and approval of GAD Plan and Budget.  The GAD Focal Person is advised to monitor and evaluate the implementation of programs and activities of the approved GPB.

3.4 	We recommended to management and to the Sanggunian Bayan Members to facilitate the creation of position for LDRRM Officer or its equivalent in accordance to Section 4.2 of JMC No. 2014-01 (NDRRMC-DILG_DBM-CSC) dated April 4, 2014.  Management should revisit the LDRRMP and ensure that performance indicators and measures are specific, measurable, achievable relevant and time bound to facilitate assessment of the progress toward the achievement of the outcomes, objectives and goals.  Moreover, the approved LDRRMP as well as the LDRRMFIP are submitted to the OCD for review and evaluation.

4.5 	We recommended that management strictly comply with the submission/transmittal of executed contracts together with supporting documents within five (5) days after its perfection to afford the audit team a prompt review and evaluation thereof.  Any defects/deficiency in result of the review shall be timely communicated to management as a tool to adopt corrective measures and/or improve their operational and procurement processes.

5.4 	We recommended that Municipal Accountant ensures that the supporting documents are complete before effecting payment to prove the validity, propriety and legality of the claim pursuant to existing law, rules and regulations. Moreover, management is advised to submit the lacking documents and comply with the other deficiencies noted to avoid audit suspension/disallowance of the transactions.

6.4 	We recommended that Municipal Accountant ensures that the supporting documents of the disbursement vouchers are complete before effecting payment to prove the regularity and validity of the claim pursuant to existing law, rules and regulations. Moreover, management is advised to submit the lacking documents and comply with the other deficiencies noted to avoid audit suspension/disallowance of the transactions.

7.5	Management explained that some of these programs were fully implemented, while other programs are on-going.. They committed to submit and liquidate all fund transfer upon completion of the projects/programs.

Fund | Amount
General Fund | P1,010,496.36
Trust Fund | 7,072,089.33
Total | P8,082,585.69

8.5 	We recommend that the Local Chief Executive create a committee to conduct physical inventory of the properties of the municipality and to prepare a report thereon to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation with that of the accounting records, pursuant to the provisions of Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.
8.6	The Sanggunian Bayan Members should facilitate the creation of position for General Services Officer (GSO) who will take custody of and accountable for all properties owned by the municipality.

9.4 	We recommended to management to fast tract the completion of all on–going and not implemented projects funded projects under the 20% Development Fund to optimize utilization thereof and timely brings the desired benefits to the intended beneficiaries.  Likewise, the concerned department is advised to monitor regularly the status of implementation of the 20% Development Plan to achieve the desired goals and benefits.

10.6	Management explained that they have already identified a site for their sanitary landfill subject to the approval of the Mines and Geosciences Bureau (MGB). They also committed to make representations on the approval of their ESWMP which was already submitted to the Department of Environment and Natural Resources (DENR).

We recommended that management adhere strictly with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 for the complete and timely submission of disbursement vouchers together with supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: The municipality incurred delays  in the submission of disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management thru the Local Solid Waste Management Board, prepare a 10 year Solid Waste Management Plan, consistent with the National Solid Waste Management Framework and submit the same to the Solid Waste Management Commission for approval required under Republic Act No. 9003, otherwise known as the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.
observations.managementAction: The municipality submitted their ESWMP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management to comply with the provisions of PCW-DILG-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2013-01 dated July 18, 2013 on the preparation, review and approval of GAD Plan and Budget.
observations.managementAction: The management submitted their GAD Plan & Budget to PPDO Cagayan but no information on the status.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 10 of the report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management formulate a Policy Guideline for contractual/casual/job order employees, taking into consideration the necessity of hiring and the reasonable number of contractual/casual/job order employees to be assigned in different offices in order to avoid excessive manpower and work duplication. 

The policy guideline to be formulated shall not, in any way, restrict the manpower requirements needed in the delivery of vital services for the implementation of the various programs and activities of the municipality and will promote optimum utilization of financial resources.
observations.managementAction: The management has formulated a Policy Guideline for contractual/ casual/job order employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the procurement of goods and services should be through the different procurement methods as set forth in the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Government Procurement Reform Act, in order to adhere to the principle of transparency, accountability, equity, efficiency and economy in its procurement process.
observations.managementAction: Management adopted the alternative methods of procurement set forth by RA 9184 and its Revised IRR due to its geographical location.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the loan granted to beneficiaries as Loan Receivables-Others to fairly present the accounts in the financial statements. Likewise, maintain Subsidiary Ledger for each beneficiary. Concerned officials and employees should closely monitor the loans to avoid non-recovery/non-collection and eventual wastage of government funds, and to ensure that the intended purpose of the program will be fully achieved.
observations.managementAction: The concerned officials and employees have implemented the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to management, thru the Local Finance Committee and all responsible officials in the preparation of the budget and utilization of funds, strictly adhere to the guidelines to ensure that the utilization of the development fund are for purposes for which it was intended.
observations.managementAction: The  programs/ projects programmed under the 20% Development Fund are now in accordance with the JMC 2011-1 guidelines.
